definition_population.content: Chronic autoimmune depigmenting skin disorder from selective melanocyte destruction; affects both sexes, any age.

sources.quote: Vitiligo, a common depigmenting skin disorder, has an estimated prevalence of 0.5-2% of the population worldwide.

epidemiology.content: Estimated global prevalence 0.5-2%; formally classified as an autoimmune disease per 2011 international consensus.

sources.quote: Vitiligo, a common depigmenting skin disorder, has an estimated prevalence of 0.5-2% of the population worldwide.

biology.content: CD8+ T cells and dendritic cells destroy melanocytes; keratinocyte IL-15 activates memory CD8+ T cells via JAK-STAT; itaconate modulates DC/CD8 axis. TMEM131L is an independently established regulator of thymocyte development and Wnt/Notch signaling; no direct TMEM131L-vitiligo literature link found (0 hits on direct search) — connection inferred from platform ranking + established T-cell developmental role.

sources.quote: dendritic cell (DC)- and CD8T cell-mediated autoimmunity is critical for destroying melanocytes in vitiligo
epidermal IL-15 expression was mainly derived from keratinocytes
we identify transmembrane protein 131-like (TMEM131L) as a novel regulator of thymocyte proliferation and demonstrate that it corresponds to a not as yet reported inhibitor of Wnt signaling.

treatment.content: Topical JAK inhibitor ruxolitinib cream (OPZELURA, NDA215309) is an approved standard-of-care option for repigmentation, distinct from oral JAKAFI (NDA202192).

sources.quote: NDA215309 OPZELURA CREAM TOPICAL (ruxolitinib), distinct from NDA202192 JAKAFI TABLET ORAL [application NDA215309; no URL field returned by drug-regulatory MCP tool -- no URL constructed]

regulatory_trials.content: 261 total interventional trials for vitiligo on ClinicalTrials.gov. Example Phase 1 trial: NCT06813027 (MSC-derived EVs).

sources.quote: Use of Allogeneic Extracellular Secretomes (EV) Derived From Umbilical Cord Mesenchymal Stromal Cells: a Phase I Open-label Safety Trial.

gaps: No direct PubMed evidence linking TMEM131L to vitiligo specifically (0 hits on direct search); connection inferred from platform ranking and TMEM131L's independently known T-cell developmental role.
Vitiligo-specific FDA label indication text for OPZELURA not independently re-confirmed beyond the Drugs@FDA product listing.
